title: Cost of Capital: Calculate the WACC if the cost of equity is 10%,...
summary: Calculate the weighted average cost of capital using equity and debt costs with capital structure weights.
prompt: Calculate the WACC if the cost of equity is 10%, the cost of debt is 5%, and the firm has a debt-to-equity ratio of 1:1. Assume a tax rate of 30%.
solution: To calculate the WACC, we use the formula: WACC = (E/V)×Re + (D/V)×Rd(1-T), where E is equity, D is debt, V is total value (E+D), Re is the cost of equity, Rd is the cost of debt, and T is the tax rate. Given a debt-to-equity ratio of 1:1, we have E = D, so V = E + D = 2E. Thus, WACC = (0.5)×(10%) + (0.5)×(5%)(1-0.3) = 0.05 + 0.0175 = 0.0675 or 6.75%.
